ground_truth.title: Best Minecraft Server Hosts [2025 UPDATED]
ground_truth.author: pelex
ground_truth.main_content:
pelex
May 26, 2025

Hello,

I wanted to come here to share a list I have created with points, pricing and my personal opinions from years of experience in the minecraft server hosting space as to the best providers out there and some you should maybe avoid. Please leave your experiences below so together we can all help each other out. This post does NOT contain any affiliate links and exists to educate and inform.

I am aware of Best Minecraft Server Hosts (Mega-Review $3 and below) and I think it is a very good list. This list will feature some of the same and some different providers I have had experiences with.

ABR Hosting (5/5) $0.99/Gb
ABR Hosting is a UK based server hosting provider. They have a growing list of server locations and a growing game library. They have a quick and active support team that can be reached by their ticket system, discord or by phone. Servers are smooth and haven't experienced any lag spikes or issues with the panel.

Locations: United States (NYC & Philadelphia), Germany (Falkenstein), Finland (Helsinki), The United Kingdom (Manchester)
Panel: Modified Pterodactyl with Mod & Plugin Installer + Built in Proxy
Benefits: Scalable plans where you can upgrade or downgrade resources whenever, DDoS Protection, No Slot Limits, Free Server Migration
Money Back Guarantee: 7 Days

CloudNord (4.5/5) $1.96/Gb
In BobFedora's post CloudNord was rated the best budget and bang for buck option amongst the hosts reviewed scoring a 9.5/10.

Locations: United States (East & West), Canada (Toronto), Australia (Sydney), Singapore, Brazil (São Paulo), India (Mumbai), United Kingdom (London), Germany (Nuremberg), Netherlands (Amsterdam)
Panel: Modified Pterodactyl Panel with Mod, ModPack & Plugin Installer + Built in Proxy
Benefits: Scalable plans where you can upgrade or downgrade resources whenever, DDoS Protection, No Slot Limits, Free Server Migration, Unmetered Disk Space (soft limit but increased if necessary)
Money Back Guarantee: 3 Days

WiseHosting (4/5) $3/Gb
WiseHosting is a minecraft server hosting company founded by YouTuber Shulkercraft. It has outstandingly positive reviews regarding its ease of use, panel features and amazing 24/7 support.

The panel features addon installers, presets for mods and plugins as well as a live player manager. They have the usual location availability for the scale of the hosting business.

Locations: United Kingdom (London), United States (Virginia & Oregon), Germany, Singapore, Australia (Sydney)
Panel: Wise Panel (custom) with Mod & Plugin Installer, advanced player manager and more.
Benefits: No slot limits, Free Server Migration, Upgrade/Downgrade at any time
Money Back Guarantee: 24hrs

Galactic Nodes (4/5)
This is a small startup minecraft server host that I am adding to the list as I found the support team to be very passionate and me and other clients I spoke to where very happy with the service they where provided. Support was quick to help with setup and very happy to assist wherever possible. The only downside I found to this host was at the moment all their billing is ran through discord but I am told that this will be changing in the very near future. They are a very bare bones hosting solution and I suggest you go in knowing how to manage and customise minecraft servers. They sell bundles that come with Bungee servers and Game Servers however they aren't fully scalable or customisable plans.

Locations:
Panel: Pterodactyl
Benefits: No slot limits, Free Server Migration
Money Back Guarantee:

PebbleHost (4/5) $1/Gb
For bang for buck PebbleHost is going to be up there with your best picks. Their servers are not the most reliable but their plans and dedicated servers provide a good resource allocation for the price.

Claims on their website like "100% server uptime" where below it says "[they] work to provide a 100% uptime on all of [their] plans," I find to be misleading with no SLA or uptime guarantee you are at the mercy of their ability to care to keep the uptime that good. A quick look at their node status show they have well in the region of hundreds of servers in a long list of locations making PebbleHost a good provide if you wish to have multiple servers in multiple locations. When checking the status page nodes range from:

1. 100% uptime and no issues
2. Not 100% uptime with servers being down for a day or more

This is a worrying pattern, yes their is a good chance that your service is completely fine and will remain online but their is a risk that you and your players are out of luck for possibly days at a time whilst a problem is solved.

Locations: Canada, Europe, Australia (Premium Only), Singapore (Premium Only), United States (Premium Only)
Panel: Modified Pterodactyl Panel with Free Subdomain Creator, Firewall and Server Importer
Benefits: Unlimited Slots, Unmetered Disk Space, DDoS Protection
Money Back Guarantee: 3 Days

GGServers (4.5/5) $3/Gb
GG Servers is one of the more expensive options when it comes to server hosting. Their standard options are $3/Gb and their premium will cost you twice as much but provides you NVMe SSD instead of their standard SSDs, it also unlocks faster DDR4 RAM and faster CPU clock speeds. However doesn't specify single thread performance which we all know is key for a minecraft server.
They are praised for their reliable and helpful customer support and scalable servers.

Their status page has been paused for the last two years so we are unable to grab accurate uptime however on their website they claim: 99.99% uptime.

Locations: United States (Premium), Canada (Montreal), United Kingdom (Premium), France (Roubaix), Germany (Premium), Finland (Premium), Singapore (Premium), Australia (Premium)
Panel: AMP (Application Management Panel) with instant plugin auto setup, automatic scan of logs and crashes, Geyser AutoSetup, Whitelist Editor
Benefits: Unlimited Slots (+$1 or free for +3 month billing), Unmetered Disk Space, DDoS Protection (varies by location)
Money Back Guarantee: 3 Days

ShockByte (3/5) $3-4/Gb
Compared to other services on this list ShockByte is on the more expensive side. They list their plans by categories: Vanilla, With Modpacks and Community Server. They have unlimited player slots but market their plans based off your play count expectations and the RAM provided. You get 25% off first payment meaning that in some cases you are paying $4/Gb. On their website, on the same page we saw them advertise 100% uptime and 99.9% uptime. It is important to note that a 99.9% uptime correlates to 8hrs a year and we have received reports of peoples servers being down for day(s) at a time. Their reviews are very negative regarding their support which shows annoyance at their AI support and only redeeming support notes being the few people praising actually being able to reach a real person after a long wait.
We wouldn't recommend this host based off of the bad customer support, server setup issues and the conflicting sales statements and pricing.

Of note, ShockByte has a warning on its TrustPilot account regarding their misleading representation of reviews.

Locations: North America, Europe, Singapore, India, Australia
Panel: Multicraft Control Panel
Benefits: Modpack & Plugin Installer, Unlimited Slots, Unlimited Disk Space, DDoS Protection
Money Back Guarantee: 3 Days

CDB
May 27, 2025

Are you maybe interested in adding some localised options as well?

OneCountry
Aug 2, 2025

I've heard things about Shockbyte, and PebbleHost was a budget option I tried awhile back. Pebble worked great! I chose Lily Host after and have been using them for awhile now though because of their CPU performance, plus pricepoint offered. Lily is much better, imo

Darthmineboy
Aug 4, 2025

This is clearly a marketing post for ABR hosting or Galactic Nodes, nothing informational about it ;)

I don't have an issue with that on its own, but you are misrepresenting the Trustpilot ratings in your favor.
- ABR Hosting has 4.1 stars, not 5
- Galactic Nodes has no reviews, why is it getting 4.5 stars in your post?
- All other hosts are receiving LESS stars in your post than on Trustpilot

Skimmed the 2025 updates - helpful, but I'd still test. The "best Minecraft server hosting" choice depends more on single-thread perf and routing than headline RAM.

ApexHosting was the best host I've ever had. They are a lil more expensive but very worth when the performance is way more over 2x for about the same specs.
Had multiple terrible experiences with shockbyte, one of them straight up deleted my files. They were running out of space lmao.

adrian97
Oct 23, 2025

I don't understand why no one mentioned NameHero. It might not be very well-known, but the price-to-performance ratio is incredible compared to others. For example: 8 GB DDR5 RAM, 100 GB NVMe storage, 3 cores - Ryzen 7950X - for just $3 per month (about $40 per year). That's almost impossible to find with other hosting providers. My friends and I have been running a server on this hosting for a few months now, and there hasn't been a single day with any issues ^.^
